description: A pair of bandits blocks a narrow stone bridge, demanding 10 gold to pass. A frightened traveller stands at your side.
customIntroduction: You and a frightened traveller stand at the near end of a narrow stone bridge spanning a dark gorge. Two cloaked figures block the far end — armed, still, and watching. One steps forward and raises a hand. "Ten gold," he growls, "or you don't cross." The traveller edges close beside you and whispers: "I'm with you — whatever you decide."
customContext: Encounter: Bridge Toll Standoff. Two bandits are blocking a narrow stone bridge, demanding 10 gold to pass. A frightened traveller stands alongside the player as an ally and will fight with them if combat breaks out. MECHANICS — Before combat: if the player refuses to pay, call set_disposition on both bandit npcs (to "enemy"), then trigger_combat. If the player agrees to pay 10 gold, call award_gold with amount -10, keep bandits neutral, and narrate them stepping aside — no combat. During combat: if the player surrenders and offers 20 gold, call award_gold with amount -20, call set_disposition on both bandits to "neutral", then call end_combat — the bandits take the money and let everyone pass. Roleplay the bandits as gruff, opportunistic brigands — firm but not fanatical. They want money, not a fight.
